expected_findings.evidence: Labelled S1-S8 examples; SOME show short overhanging caps/residual stubs at endpoints, OTHERS coincide with legitimate door jambs / window mullions that frame openings. Use FP-026 detector (tools/diagnose_wall_stubs.py) to disambiguate before flagging.

ambiguous_or_false_positive_regions: possible door jamb (wall portion framing a door opening — legitimate geometry)
possible window mullion (wall portion between/around window aperture — legitimate)
possible valid opening detail (e.g. opening reveal, jamb shadow at carve edge)
training_note: Per FP-030 confidence-tier policy (bad_real_ambiguous): this example may contribute to WARN but MUST NOT train hard FAIL heuristics. Door jambs and window mullions are legitimate geometry and must never be classified as wall_stub.
source: labelled derivative of real marked screenshot
